seoTitle: How to Connect WordPress to GitHub for Version Control
metaDescription: Link WordPress to GitHub to manage theme and plugin code with version control. This guide covers repo setup, pushing files, and automating deployment.
intro: Managing WordPress code through GitHub gives you version control, a safety net for changes, and the option to automate deployments instead of manually uploading files. This guide covers creating a GitHub repository for your site, connecting it to WordPress, and pushing your theme and plugin files up. It works across common hosting setups like cPanel, Cloudways, Hostinger, and local environments like LocalWP or XAMPP.

steps.heading: Create a GitHub repository for your site
steps.body: Set up a new repository on GitHub to hold your WordPress theme and plugin files before you connect anything to your hosting.

steps.heading: Connect WordPress to GitHub
steps.body: Link your WordPress installation to the new repository using the method that fits your hosting setup, whether that's a plugin, SSH keys, or a manual connection.

steps.heading: Push theme and plugin files to GitHub
steps.body: Upload your existing theme and plugin files into the repository so version history starts tracking from your current site state.

steps.heading: Set up automated deployment
steps.body: Configure auto-deployment so future changes pushed to GitHub can flow through to your WordPress hosting without manual file transfers.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot common Git errors
steps.body: Watch for permission issues and file update problems, which are common early on, and resolve them before relying on the workflow for real updates.

faq.question: Do I need to know Git commands to connect WordPress to GitHub?
faq.answer: Basic Git knowledge helps, but the tutorial focuses on beginner-friendly methods so you don't need to be an experienced developer to get started.

faq.question: Does this work with any WordPress hosting provider?
faq.answer: Yes, the approach works across common setups including cPanel hosting, Cloudways, Namecheap, Hostinger, LocalWP, and XAMPP.

faq.question: Can I automatically deploy changes from GitHub to my live site?
faq.answer: Yes, once the connection is set up you can configure automated deployment so pushes to GitHub update your WordPress hosting directly.

faq.question: What if I get permission errors when pushing files?
faq.answer: Permission and file update issues are common when first connecting WordPress to GitHub, and the tutorial covers how to fix the most frequent ones.
